task: I need to send a quick note about a safety issue that came up this morning, but I want it to stay brief and easy to act on. Can you help me draft something I could send right away, plus a second version that breaks the next steps into plain, simple pieces if needed?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely. Here are two ready-to-send versions.

**Short note**
> Safety issue came up this morning and needs to be corrected before work continues in that area. Please stop, fix the condition, and confirm back once it’s done so we can move ahead safely.

**Plain-step version**
> Safety issue came up this morning and the area needs attention before work continues.  
> 1) Stop work in the affected area.  
> 2) Fix the condition now.  
> 3) Check it’s safe before restarting.  
> 4) Confirm back when it’s done.

If you want, I can make it sound more like a **text message**, **email**, or **field notice**.

user_simulator.text:
Too generic. That’s basically a template, not a note I can actually use without editing half of it.

Give me something tighter and more work-like. I need it to sound like a real safety note, not a polite reminder. And if you’re going to do the step version, make it simple enough that a foreman can read it once and act without guessing.

Use this kind of structure:
- what the issue is
- where it is
- what needs to stop
- what has to happen before restart
- who confirms

Keep it brief. No fluff.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are two tighter, work-ready versions you can send as-is.

**Short safety note**
> Safety issue reported this morning at **[location]**: **[issue]**. Stop work in that area now. Do not restart until the condition is corrected, the area is checked, and **[name/role]** confirms it is clear to proceed.

**Plain-step version**
> Safety issue at **[location]** this morning: **[issue]**.  
> 1) Stop work in the affected area.  
> 2) Correct the condition.  
> 3) Recheck the area before restart.  
> 4) **[name/role]** gives the go-ahead before work resumes.

If you want, I can also turn this into a **harder stop-work tone** or a **site notice version** with the same structure.

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still leaves too much blank. I don’t want brackets and placeholders in something I’m sending from the field.

Give me one version that sounds like an actual site note, with the parts filled in generically enough to use right now. And make the step version even cleaner — no extra wording, just the action.

Need:
- issue
- location
- stop work
- fix/check before restart
- who signs off

Keep it blunt.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here are two field-ready versions you can send now.

**Site note**
> Safety issue identified this morning at the north work area near the loading dock: unsecured materials and an unsafe walking surface. Stop work in that area now. Do not restart until the condition is corrected, the area is rechecked, and the foreman confirms it is clear to proceed.

**Plain-step version**
> 1) Stop work in the north work area near the loading dock.  
> 2) Fix the unsecured materials and walking surface.  
> 3) Recheck the area before restart.  
> 4) Foreman confirms clearance.

If you want, I can make the same note into a **hard stop-work notice** or a **subcontractor-facing version**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That works. Site note is usable, and the step version is clean enough for a foreman to read once and move.

No more brackets, no soft language. This is fine.
